parameters.name: mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Controls where text is generated from. Either from the 'Text' parameter, or a table provided via the 'Specification ' parameter.

parameters.name: text
parameters.label: Text
parameters.description: When in 'Text' mode, this specifies the text that will be rendered.

parameters.name: specdat
parameters.label: Specification DAT
parameters.description: In this mode, the Text parameter is ignored and data is taken from a table given in the Specification parameter. Each row of the is a separate line of text, which can have different transforms, alignm

parameters.name: specchop
parameters.label: Specification CHOP
parameters.description: Allows use of data to set parameters of text blocks defined in the Specification . Each sample of the corresponds to the data rows in the Specification . Only numerical data such as , etc can be provi

parameters.name: sorted
parameters.label: Sort by Depth
parameters.description: Only available when using a Specification . When enabled, lines in the specification are rendered from back to front relative to the current camera to allow for proper alpha blending. Note: sorting a

parameters.name: formatcodes
parameters.label: Formatting Codes
parameters.description: Enables the use of formatting codes in the text. For example, the code {#color(255, 0, 0);} will turn all text afterwards on that line red. See for all available codes.

parameters.name: escapeseq
parameters.label: Parse Escape Sequences
parameters.description: When enabled, '\t' sequences will be converted to tabs and '\r' or '\n' to line breaks. This feature is only enabled when using the Specification . For Text mode, python expressions should be used whe

parameters.name: smartpunct
parameters.label: Smart Punctuation
parameters.description: Enables the use of smart punctuation. For example, pairs of quotes will be replaced with appropriate angled quotes, 3 periods will be replaced with an ellipses character and two hypens will become an

parameters.name: wordwrap
parameters.label: Word Wrap
parameters.description: Wrap lines of text if they go beyond the width of the layout box.

parameters.name: widthaffectedbyfov
parameters.label: Width Affected by FOV/OrthoWidth
parameters.description: With this off, looking at a rendered image of a certain resolution, the size of the text will remain the same # of pixels wide as you change the field-of-view or ortho width. With this parameter on (d

parameters.name: facecam
parameters.label: Face Camera
parameters.description: Toggle this on to make the text face the camera no matter what the camera orientation.

parameters.name: depthScaleSCurve
parameters.label: Depth Scale S-Curve
parameters.description: With this off, the size of the text is inversely proportional to the distance from the camera, that is perspective based sizing. With the toggle on, an S-curve determines the scaling of the text based

parameters.name: depthDistanceNear
parameters.label: Depth Distance Near
parameters.description: Specify a near plane with a certain distance from the camera. Used for Depth Scale S-Curve.

parameters.name: depthDistanceFar
parameters.label: Depth Distance Far
parameters.description: Specify a far plane with a certain distance from the camera. Used for Depth Scale S-Curve.

parameters.name: depthScaleNear
parameters.label: Depth Scale Near
parameters.description: When the camera distance is equal to the value of 'Depth Distance Near' parameter, the text will be scaled by this value. Used for Depth Scale S-Curve.

parameters.name: depthScaleFar
parameters.label: Depth Scale Far
parameters.description: When the camera distance is equal to the value of 'Depth Distance Far' parameter, the text will be scaled by this value. Used for Depth Scale S-Curve.

parameters.name: depthScaleBias
parameters.label: Depth Scale Bias
parameters.description: Moves the S Curve bias backward or forward for interpolation of the depth scale factor between the near and far planes. Used for Depth Scale S-Curve.

parameters.name: depthScaleSteepness
parameters.label: Depth Scale Steepness
parameters.description: Controls the steepness of the S Curve for interpolation of the depth scale factor between the near and far planes. The higher the value of the steepness, you will notice more dramatic changes (higher

parameters.name: depthScaleLinearize
parameters.label: Depth Scale Linearize
parameters.description: Control the amount of curvature in the S Curve for interpolation of the depth scale factor between the near and far planes.

parameters.name: lifttowardscam
parameters.label: Lift Towards Camera
parameters.description: For text that may be rendered on top of filled polygons or along their edges, they may be cut off because they are rendered in the same place. To make it look good, you want to lift the text towards t

parameters.name: font
parameters.label: Font
parameters.description: Select the font to be used from the dropdown menu. Available fonts are those that have been registered with the operating system. There may be a delay when selecting fonts that have not been used befo

parameters.name: fontfile
parameters.label: Font File
parameters.description: Specify a font file to be used for rendering the text. This option can be used for fonts that are not registered with the operating system and do not appear in the drop down menu.

parameters.name: bold
parameters.label: Bold
parameters.description: Display the text in .

parameters.name: italic
parameters.label: Italic
parameters.description: Display the text in italics.

parameters.name: fontsize
parameters.label: Font Size
parameters.description: The font size. This is in the same units as the geometry space. So it's arbitrary based on the size of your scene.

parameters.name: tracking
parameters.label: Tracking
parameters.description: Sets the horizontal spacing between characters, where 0 is default spacing, > 0 is increased spacing and < 0 is decreased spacing.

parameters.name: skew
parameters.label: Skew
parameters.description: Tilts the top of the characters forwards or backwards relative to the baseline.

parameters.name: horzstretch
parameters.label: Horz Stretch
parameters.description: Horizontally stretch the characters relative to their current alignment.

parameters.name: linespacing
parameters.label: Line Spacing
parameters.description: Adjust spacing between lines. The value is a multiplier of the default spacing defined by the font.

parameters.name: fontalpha
parameters.label: Font Alpha
parameters.description: The alpha value for the font.

parameters.name: layoutanchoru
parameters.label: Layout Box Anchor U
parameters.description: Along with the Box Anchor V, this allows shifting the layout box from it's current position left/down. It also controls how rotations will pivot around the layout box, so a 0.5, 0.5 anchor will cause

parameters.name: layoutanchorv
parameters.label: Layout Box Anchor V
parameters.description: The V component of the Box Anchor pair.

parameters.name: cliptolayoutbox
parameters.label: Clip to Layout Box
parameters.description: When enabled, text that falls outside of the layout box will not be drawn.

parameters.name: alignx
parameters.label: Horizontal Align
parameters.description: Controls the horizontal alignment of the text.

parameters.name: aligny
parameters.label: Vertical Align
parameters.description: Controls the vertical alignment of the text.

parameters.name: alignymode
parameters.label: Vertical Align Mode
parameters.description: Controls how the alignment is calculated for vertical alignment.

parameters.name: xord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: This allows you to specify the order in which the changes to your will take place. Changing the Transform Order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order

parameters.name: scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: This field allows you to change the size of an uniformly along the three axes.

parameters.name: parentxformsrc
parameters.label: Parent Transform Source
parameters.description: Select what position is used as the transform source for this obejct. Can be one of "Parent (Hierarchy)", "Specify Parent Object", or "World Origin".

parameters.name: parentobject
parameters.label: Parent Object
parameters.description: Allows the location of the object to be constrained to any other object whose path is specified in this parameter.

parameters.name: lookat
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Allows you to orient this by naming another 3D you would like it to , or point to. Once you have designated this to look at, it will continue to face that , even if you move it. This is useful if, for

parameters.name: forwarddir
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description: Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

parameters.name: lookup
parameters.label: Look At Up Vector
parameters.description: When specifying a , it is possible to specify an up vector for the lookat. Without using an up vector, it is possible to get poor animation when the lookat , for example, passes through the Y axis of

parameters.name: pathsop
parameters.label: Path SOP
parameters.description: Names the that functions as the path you want this to move along. For instance, you can name a that provides a path for the camera to follow.

parameters.name: roll
parameters.label: Roll
parameters.description: Using the angle control you can specify a 's rotation as it animates along the path.

parameters.name: pos
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: This parameter lets you specify the of the along the path. The values you can enter for this parameter range from to , where equals the starting point and equals the end point of the path. The value s

parameters.name: pathorient
parameters.label: Orient along Path
parameters.description: If this option is selected, the will be oriented along the path. The positive Z axis of the will be pointing down the path.

parameters.name: bank
parameters.label: Auto-Bank Factor
parameters.description: The rolls the based on the curvature of the path at its current position. To turn off auto-banking, set the bank scale to .

parameters.label: Apply Pre-Transform

parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: pscale

parameters.description: Refer to the documentation on Xform page for more information.

parameters.name: pcommit
parameters.label: Commit to Main Transform
parameters.description: This button will copy the transform from this page to the main Xform page, and reset this page's transform.

parameters.name: xformmatrixop
